concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Turkey's urban landscape. Istanbul holds 13.4% of tracked urban population across 711 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Istanbul & Bursa
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Istanbul and Bursa (93 km apart, combined population 17,542,833). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 93 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Turkey presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with Istanbul holding just 13.4% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (Istanbul → Bursa, 93 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
